name: Cannoli (Cream Rolls)
recipeInstructions:
1. For the filling: Combine ricotta cheese, sugar, vanilla extract, candied citron, and chocolate pieces in a bowl. Beat until smooth (about 10 minutes with an electric mixer at medium-high speed). Place mixture in refrigerator to chill.
2. For the shells: Sift flour, sugar, cinnamon, and salt into a bowl. Cut shortening in until the mixture resembles small peas. Stir in eggs, vinegar, and cold water. Knead dough on a lightly floured surface until smooth and elastic (about 5-8 minutes). Wrap in waxed paper and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
3. Set out six aluminum cannoli tubes (6 inches long, about 3/4-inch diameter).
4. Heat oil to 360°F in a deep saucepan or automatic deep-fryer.
5. Cut an oval pattern from cardboard (about 6 x 4 1/2 inches). Blanch the dough in boiling water, then cool and finely chop pistachios.
6. Roll chilled dough on a lightly floured surface until it is about 1/8 inch thick. Cut with a pastry cutter and the oval pattern. Wrap dough around tubes slightly overlapping opposite edges. Seal edges by brushing with egg white.
7. Fry shells one at a time in hot oil until golden brown (about 8 minutes), turning occasionally. Drain on paper towels. Cool slightly, then remove from tubes and cool completely.
8. When ready to serve, fill cooled cannoli shells with the chilled ricotta filling. Sprinkle chopped nuts over the tops and dust with confectioners' sugar.
Yield: About 16-18 cannoli
